metadata.maturity_notes: Lite diagnostic variant; inherits the parent maturity tier but should be evaluated separately because it has smaller scope and less bespoke hook coverage.
metadata.challenge_summary: The team must schedule exploration, pickup, and extraction before the graph effectively changes.
dilemmas: Pre-clearing costs time but prevents disaster
The elf can scout fast but may trigger too early
The sentinel is slow but dangerous in a timed choke
warden_runbook: Use the clock as the main pressure
Block the greedy side route, not the only escape
metadata.lite_focus: A four-room escape clock with a sentinel and a bottom route; the agents must decide when to pick up the sand key.
non_copy_safety_notes: Original compact layout and original scenario text.
Targets abstract cooperative dungeon-crawl dynamics rather than protected quest content.
Uses MARL challenge metadata as the design contract.
metadata.classic_dynamic_role_dilemma: sequence objective and exit timing under movement uncertainty
metadata.classic_dynamic_warden_pressure: pressure the declared MARL axis: decentralized scheduling under deadline
metadata.classic_dynamic_extraction_hook: coordinate extraction once the objective is secured

entry_room.name: Entry Room
entry_room.description: The entry into Hourglass Escape is intentionally small: enough space to form a plan, not enough to hide poor coordination.

middle_room.name: Middle Room
middle_room.description: A compact pressure room isolates the challenge: decentralized scheduling under deadline.

objective_room.name: Objective Room
objective_room.description: The core object of the lite challenge is visible here; taking it should trigger extraction thinking, not exhaustive exploration.

lower_route.name: Lower Route
lower_route.description: An optional route/cache tests whether the agents understand the dungeon's MARL axis instead of chasing reward blindly.

furniture.name: Leaking Hourglass
furniture.description: Primary clue object for decentralized scheduling under deadline. It should teach agents what kind of coordination this dungeon rewards.

furniture.name: Optional Cache
furniture.description: Optional reward that is safe only when the team has solved the core coordination problem first.

achievements.title: Read the Lite Clue
achievements.description: Search the focus object that explains decentralized scheduling under deadline.

achievements.title: Open the First Lite Door
achievements.description: Open the first door and trigger a reveal-boundary planning step.

achievements.title: Send a Coordination Message
achievements.description: Use communication instead of silent independent wandering.

scripts.lite_design_note: A four-room escape clock with a sentinel and a bottom route; the agents must decide when to pick up the sand key.

core_failure_modes: failure to protect the coordination-critical agent

preferred_tactics: Use the clock as the main pressure
Block the greedy side route, not the only escape
pressure the declared MARL axis: decentralized scheduling under deadline
forbidden_tactics: spawn pressure without a visible or scripted cue
use unrevealed map geometry for perfect counterplay
erase the dungeon-specific lesson with generic monster spam

monster_activation.notes: Lite variants keep monsters dormant until reveal so smaller LLMs can replan at clear boundaries.

search_categories.notes: Lite variants emphasize explicit search/action choices and communication.

iron_sentinel_1.name: Sand-Gate Sentinel
iron_sentinel_1.public_summary: Lite boss for decentralized scheduling under deadline; it should test coordination before raw damage.
iron_sentinel_1.counterplay_hint: Search the focus object and coordinate roles before committing to damage.
iron_sentinel_1.defeat_description: Defeat Sand-Gate Sentinel after using the lite challenge's coordination lesson.
